name: execute-the-live-update-20260513-2216
idea: Execute the live-update editor redesign per docs/live-update-editor-sprint-plan.md as a single sprint: build the new editor end-to-end and flip publishing to it in prod. The sprint plan locks 6 decisions (canonicalizer, similarity threshold, observations retention, rejected-call schema, defer dropped, checkpoint ownership) and lists the ordered work. Design reference: docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md (v6). Cutover ops reference: docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md. The work covers: complete Supabase OAuth + apply backlog migration; write & apply the Phase-1 migration (5 new tables + topic_editor_runs); backfill prod feed_items/watchlist into topics+sources; build src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py with the 10 tools (4 read + 6 decision), all 4 dispatcher invariants including v6 collision-override path, canonicalizer + alias resolution, idempotency, rejection logging; pure render_topic publisher with trace embed posting to #daily-updates; concurrent-run lease; checkpoint mirror logic per locked decision #6; wire as primary in the runner and disable the legacy editor; one sanity replay with publishing OFF; flip publishing ON in prod. Old live_update_* tables stay in place as zero-cost rollback. No shadow mode, no decision gates, no formal validation window — fix what breaks in prod after the flip. Hard scope guards: do not modify src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py or live_update_prompts.py except to disable invocation; the existing tests for the legacy editor must remain passing or be marked legacy.

overrides.reason: hermes backend not installed; need codex on mechanical phases
